q: How many wheels does a car usually have?
Which vehicle flies with wings and jet engines?
exp: A commercial plane cruises at around 10,000 metres.
Trains run on two steel rails.

q: Which vehicle do we use to travel on water?
exp: Boats float thanks to buoyancy (Archimedes' principle).

q: How many wheels does a bicycle have?
exp: A bicycle is powered by your legs, with no engine.

q: Which emergency vehicle puts out fires?
exp: It carries water, hoses and ladders.

q: Which vehicle rushes patients to hospital?
exp: Ambulances use sirens and lights to get priority in traffic.

q: Which vehicle carries many people around the city with lots of seats?
exp: Buses are part of public transport.

q: Which two-wheeled vehicle has an engine and needs a helmet?
exp: The helmet protects your head in a fall.
A submarine fills tanks with water to dive.
A rocket needs huge speed to escape gravity.

q: Which vehicle has rotors on top and can hover?
exp: A helicopter takes off and lands vertically.
